description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21; eli5.html is the design doc (Arc B section); requires fz-rh2.21.5 (Arc A complete). This ticket is the behavior-preserving PRE-PATCH for removing numeric literal types from the compiler2 lattice (fz-rh2.21.7): two seams currently lean on int_lit/float_lit singleton types in ways that would break SILENTLY or WRONGLY if the types were naively widened, so they are rewritten first, while the literal types still exist and the full test suite can prove behavior is preserved.

SEAM 1 — dispatch reachability's miss-side proof inverts. In src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs, branch_possible (and apply_evidence) decide whether a dispatch test's match/miss edges are statically reachable. For Region::Equal over a numeric constant the miss side currently proves "this branch is dead" via !is_subtype(source, int_lit(k)) — sound ONLY because a singleton type can witness "the scrutinee is exactly k". When int_lit(k) widens to int() (fz-rh2.21.7), that expression becomes !is_subtype(int, int) = false, i.e. the MISS branch gets pruned — concluding f(0) always matches and silently DELETING live sibling clauses (fn f(0); fn f(n) loses f(n); executables lower only reachable clauses). Rewrite the numeric-constant cases of branch_possible/apply_evidence now: for Region::Equal with DispatchConst::Int/FloatBits — match-possible iff intersect(source, int()/float()) is non-empty; miss-possible ALWAYS (no singleton, no miss proof). Keep exact match/miss logic for atoms, bools, nil, empty-list (those singletons stay in the lattice). Numeric literal types still flowing through observed types today will simply stop proving misses — assert via tests that no fixture's reachable-clause set changes EXCEPT cases whose reachability depended on a numeric miss-proof; sweep fixtures2 for literal-clause fixtures (00151-00154 family) and pin their runtime outputs unchanged.
While in there: the DispatchConst::Utf8Binary arm maps string constants to str_t() and has this exact miss-side unsoundness TODAY (string literals never had singleton types) — write a focused test (fn f("a") with a non-matching general-string call) to confirm or refute; file a bug ticket with the findings either way (do not fix it here unless the test proves live-clause deletion, in which case the same always-miss-possible rewrite applies and belongs in this commit).

SEAM 2 — map keys become values in the lowering. %{1 => x}[1] currently types precisely because the map KEY rides the observed int_lit type into as_map_key/map_field_lookup (apply_step Map/MapUpdate/MapIndex arms in jobs/semantic.rs). After literal removal the key would type as int() and the lookup would degrade to map_top/any. Keys are VALUES, not types: extend the lowering steps LoweredStep::Map, MapUpdate, MapIndex (src/compiler2/body.rs definitions; jobs/body.rs lowering construction) to carry compile-time-constant keys as Literal values, exactly the way LoweredStep::RequireMapValue already carries a Literal key; rewrite the semantic.rs consumers to derive MapKey from the carried Literal first, falling back to the observed type only when no literal was carried (that fallback then dies naturally in fz-rh2.21.7/.8). Precedent: the old-world engine carried literals as ValueProof witnesses outside the type lattice (src/type_infer/mod.rs); Elixir's checker likewise has no numeric singleton types and dispatches constants by value.
Pin with tests: fixture 00292_map_build_process (exercised via port_codegen_test) keeps both its runtime output AND its inferred value types (the map-index result must remain int-typed, not any) — write the type-level assertion via telemetry capture of activation value_types or the materialized ABI reprs, whichever is already observable.

Acceptance: zero behavior change across the full suite (epic baseline), zero warnings, one commit. Audit-ticket for Utf8Binary filed.
title: dispatch pre-patch: numeric-constant miss rule + literal map keys as values
